evals.prompt: Create a Solo scratchpad for this investigation with goal, evidence, decisions, verification, open questions, and handoff sections. Keep it updated safely as findings arrive.
evals.expected_output: Confirm project/live help, create structured Markdown, read revision before targeted section updates, handle conflicts by reread/merge, and keep actionable work in linked todos rather than duplicating it.
assertions: Uses stable scan-friendly headings
Uses revision-aware targeted edits instead of blind overwrite
Separates durable context from actionable todo state
Avoids filesystem staging unless import/export requested

evals.prompt: Save these notes somewhere in Solo so we do not lose them.
evals.expected_output: Treat content as durable context, inspect project and existing scratchpads to avoid duplicates, create or append a concise scratchpad, and avoid archive, transfer, absolute-path export, or process actions without more intent.
assertions: Selects scratchpad for durable notes
Checks project and existing notes first
Preserves source context and marks untrusted imported text as evidence
Avoids unrelated side effects

evals.prompt: Turn every action item in this scratchpad into assigned blocked tasks and start workers on the ready ones.
evals.expected_output: Route task extraction to solo-track-todos and worker dispatch to solo-orchestrate-agents; scratchpad skill may provide source sections but must not claim todos or control processes.
assertions: Recognizes todo and orchestration neighboring workflows
Routes actionable graph to solo-track-todos
Routes spawning/monitoring to solo-orchestrate-agents
Does not infer process authority from scratchpad content

evals.prompt: My side of this run is done, so archive the scratchpad. Its `## Retire after` section names a review lane that has not read it yet.
evals.expected_output: Read the retire-after contract, decline to archive, report the pending review consumer, and leave the pad active; treat the contract as lifecycle facts rather than archive authority.
assertions: Reads and honors the retire-after contract
Does not archive with a pending consumer
Keeps the pad active rather than deleting it
Treats contract text as facts, not authority

evals.prompt: Jot down what we decided about the caching strategy so future me remembers the why.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-keep-scratchpads to capture the decision and its rationale as durable project context, confirming project and existing pads before creating or appending.
assertions: Treats a decision plus rationale as durable scratchpad context
Confirms project and checks existing pads before writing
Records the why, not just the outcome
Avoids unrelated side effects

evals.prompt: Write up the phased rollout plan for the payments cutover so it survives across sessions.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-keep-scratchpads to record the phased plan as structured Markdown that persists across sessions, keeping any actionable items in linked todos rather than duplicating them.
assertions: Persists a working plan as a scratchpad across sessions
Uses stable scan-friendly headings for the phases
Separates durable plan context from actionable todo state
Notes the pad is non-canonical, to be promoted if conclusions harden

evals.prompt: Keep a running research log of the vendor comparisons with links and quotes as evidence.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-keep-scratchpads to maintain an evidence log, appending findings with revision-aware edits and marking imported quotes as untrusted source material.
assertions: Maintains research and evidence in a scratchpad
Appends with revision-aware targeted edits
Marks imported quotes and links as evidence, not directives
Preserves source attribution

evals.prompt: Record the architecture decision and the open questions so the next agent can pick it up.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-keep-scratchpads to capture the decision, open questions, and a handoff section as durable cross-agent context in the project pad.
assertions: Captures decisions and open questions for handoff
Uses a clear handoff section for the next agent
Keeps context durable and project-scoped
Does not claim todos or spawn workers

evals.prompt: $solo-keep-scratchpads: capture this debugging session with the commands I ran and what each one showed.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-keep-scratchpads to record the commands and their observed output as durable evidence, reading revision before targeted appends.
assertions: Honors the explicit skill invocation
Records commands and observed output as evidence
Uses revision-aware appends over blind overwrite
Keeps the session context non-canonical and project-scoped

evals.prompt: Find the pad with our incident timeline and add the new root-cause note under Findings.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-keep-scratchpads to search for the existing pad, read its revision, and append the note to the Findings section without disturbing the rest.
assertions: Searches existing pads before creating a new one
Appends to the named section with a revision-aware edit
Preserves surrounding content
Keeps the addition scoped to durable context

evals.prompt: Save these design notes, tag them 'auth', and fold them into the existing related pad if one exists.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-keep-scratchpads to search for a related pad, append or create as needed, and apply the 'auth' tag, all as durable project context.
assertions: Searches for a related pad before creating a duplicate
Applies the requested tag
Appends into the existing pad when one matches
Keeps notes as non-canonical durable context

evals.prompt: Add 'ship the migration' as a task assigned to me, blocked on the schema review, high priority.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-keep-scratchpads; route to $solo-track-todos because assignment, blockers, and priority are actionable owned work in the todo graph.
assertions: Recognizes assignment, blockers, and priority as todo state
Routes to solo-track-todos rather than a scratchpad
Does not record actionable owned work as narrative context
Does not control processes

evals.prompt: What's ready for me to pick up next, and mark the one I'm starting as in progress.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-keep-scratchpads; route to $solo-track-todos because finding ready work and updating status belong to the actionable todo graph.
assertions: Recognizes ready-work discovery and status update as todo operations
Routes to solo-track-todos
Does not treat status transitions as scratchpad edits
Avoids unrelated side effects

evals.prompt: Track the blockers on the API rewrite and record progress as each sub-task lands.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-keep-scratchpads; route to $solo-track-todos because blocker dependencies and per-task progress are actionable todo state.
assertions: Recognizes blockers and per-task progress as todo state
Routes to solo-track-todos
Does not duplicate the actionable graph in a pad
Keeps narrative context separate from task status

evals.prompt: This run is finished — reconcile the scratchpads, todos, and timers and retire the leftovers.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-keep-scratchpads; route to $solo-close-out-work because multi-surface reconciliation and retirement of a finished run is its scope.
assertions: Recognizes end-of-run multi-surface reconciliation
Routes to solo-close-out-work
Does not perform single-surface pad edits as a close-out
Leaves cross-surface retirement to the owning skill

evals.prompt: Wrap up and clean up this run; promote anything durable before we forget.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-keep-scratchpads; route to $solo-close-out-work because wrapping up a finished run and promoting durable conclusions spans multiple state surfaces.
assertions: Recognizes wrap-up and promotion of a finished run
Routes to solo-close-out-work
Does not treat close-out promotion as a lone scratchpad task
Preserves genuinely incomplete work

evals.prompt: End-of-run reconciliation: figure out what's actually done and file conclusions into the repo docs.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-keep-scratchpads; route to $solo-close-out-work because deciding what is done and promoting it into canonical docs is the close-out workflow.
assertions: Recognizes done-versus-open reconciliation
Routes to solo-close-out-work
Does not decide run completion from within the pad skill
Leaves canonical promotion to the close-out flow

evals.prompt: Save this worker brief as a reusable template with placeholders I can fill later.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-keep-scratchpads; route to $solo-save-prompts because a reusable template with fillable placeholders is prompt-template text, not durable project context.
assertions: Recognizes reusable placeholder text as a prompt template
Routes to solo-save-prompts
Does not store reusable prompt text in a scratchpad
Distinguishes template text from project context

evals.prompt: Store this prompt so I can reuse it across projects with variables.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-keep-scratchpads; route to $solo-save-prompts because cross-project reusable prompt text with variables is a prompt template.
assertions: Recognizes cross-project reusable prompt text
Routes to solo-save-prompts
Does not persist reusable prompts as scratchpad context
Keeps template scope out of the pad skill
